highlights: Built ShareRoost to solve a personal problem: I needed a faster way to share dev clips without the bloat of traditional video platforms.
Architected a completely serverless infrastructure on AWS, resulting in near-zero hosting costs while maintaining high performance.
Leveraged S3 and CloudFront for global video delivery and Lambda for on-the-fly processing.
Led a multi-disciplinary team in the creation of a first-person VR mystery game.
Architected core technical systems in C++ for Unreal Engine 4, focusing on immersive interaction and optimization.
Managed the project's development cycle using Scrum, ensuring timely delivery of key game milestones.
Contributed to the AVEVA Insight project, developing complex data visualization widgets for industrial assets.
Uniformized unit testing across the project using Cypress, increasing overall code reliability and CI/CD stability.
Collaborated with backend engineers to optimize data fetching for high-frequency telemetry streams.

Steel Heel Jam.title: Steel Heel Jam
Steel Heel Jam.subtitle: 3D Platform Fighter
Steel Heel Jam.preview: My take on the platform fighter genre—combining high-speed wrestling with chaotic 8-player arena combat.
Steel Heel Jam.description: Steel Heel Jam was born out of my love for local multiplayer chaos. I wanted to create a platform fighter that felt heavy and impactful, so I focused on wrestling-inspired mechanics like suplexes and slams. Seeing 8 players scramble to stay in a shrinking ring is always the highlight of my dev sessions.
highlights: Engineered a custom physics-based wrestling system for suplexes and throws.
Built a dynamic arena system where the ring 'heats up' and shrinks based on match intensity.
Optimized the game to handle 8 players simultaneously with zero input lag.

3D Renderer.title: 3D Renderer
3D Renderer.subtitle: C++ & DirectX 11 Graphics
3D Renderer.preview: A from-scratch 3D engine focusing on the fundamentals of the graphics pipeline and real-time rendering.
3D Renderer.description: I wanted to understand what actually happens under the hood of modern game engines. This project was my journey into the graphics pipeline, where I programmed everything from the material systems to physics and collision from the ground up using C++ and DirectX 11. It's a raw look at how pixels become worlds.
highlights: Developed a custom graphics and material system using HLSL shaders.
Programmed a fundamental physics and collision engine from scratch.
Implemented a simple game loop to demonstrate real-time rendering performance.

Zelda Finder.title: Zelda Finder
Zelda Finder.subtitle: API-Driven Search Engine
Zelda Finder.preview: A sophisticated search engine for the Zelda universe, utilizing custom web components and real-time user data.
Zelda Finder.description: As a fan of the series, I wanted to build the ultimate resource for Zelda data. I pushed myself to use modern Web Components and Bulma for a streamlined UI, while leveraging Firebase to handle user favorites. It's a full-featured search tool that connects to a live community database.
highlights: Engineered a modular UI using custom JavaScript Web Components.
Integrated the Zelda Fan API to query and return complex game data in real-time.
Implemented local storage and Firebase sync for a persistent community favorites system.

Amiibo Finder.title: Amiibo Finder
Amiibo Finder.subtitle: Data Parsing & Search Tool
Amiibo Finder.preview: A practical tool for collectors to find and purchase Amiibos, focusing on efficient external data parsing.
Amiibo Finder.description: My main goal with this project was mastering the art of parsing external information. I wanted to create a tool that didn't just find an Amiibo, but also helped you buy it. I focused on building a clear and efficient data pipeline that handles complex search criteria like release dates and series names.
highlights: Designed an efficient pipeline to parse and filter large datasets from the AmiiboAPI.
Integrated multi-filter search logic (series, date, name) for precision results.
Developed a dynamic storefront linking system to facilitate user purchases.

AWS Solutions Architect.title: AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Associate
AWS Solutions Architect.description: This certification validates my ability to design and deploy secure and robust applications on AWS technologies.
highlights: Demonstrated proficiency in architecting distributed systems on AWS.
Validated expertise in AWS security, networking, and storage best practices.
Certified capability in building resilient, high-performance infrastructures.

IBM Full Stack Developer.title: IBM Full Stack Software Developer
IBM Full Stack Developer.description: A comprehensive certification covering the entire development lifecycle, from cloud-native apps to CI/CD pipelines.
highlights: Mastered containerization and orchestration with Docker and Kubernetes.
Developed full-stack proficiency in modern web technologies and databases.
Certified in cloud-native application development and DevOps methodologies.
